summary:
This document outlines the planned evolution of DiffDoc, a tool that automatically tracks code changes, generates plain-English summaries of those changes, and makes them searchable across a project or organization.

**Business Behavior & Rules**
- The tool will enforce documentation standards by automatically generating summaries whenever files change.
- It will include health checks to verify configuration, connectivity, and data integrity before processing.
- Security and compliance rules will be configurable, allowing teams to choose encryption standards and control how file content is identified.
- Governance rules will track metadata like file owners, modules, and tags, enabling audit trails and access filtering.
- CI/CD pipelines will be able to block merges if changed files lack summaries, ensuring documentation keeps pace with code.

**User Impact**
- Developers will see faster, more reliable processing with automatic retries, parallel execution, and the ability to pause and resume long runs without losing progress.
- Users will get clearer, customizable summaries (brief, balanced, or detailed) with adjustable length limits.
- Search results will improve by combining exact keyword matching with meaning-based matching, prioritizing relevant project areas, and showing short proof snippets alongside answers.
- Complex questions will be broken down and answered step-by-step, with confidence ratings shown when results are uncertain.
- A preview mode will let users see what will change before applying it, and a comparison view will highlight how summaries evolve over time.

**Data Movement & Storage**
- The system will track which files have been processed, skipped, updated, or failed, providing clear counts for monitoring.
- Processed summaries and search indexes will be stored persistently, with automatic cleanup of outdated or unused data.
- Data can be exported in standard formats (Markdown, JSON, CSV) for reporting or integration with other business systems.
- Multiple repositories can be combined into a single, organization-wide searchable index.

**Visible Outcomes**
- A dashboard-style status view showing file counts, index freshness, and system health.
- Automated comments on pull requests that explain business impact and technical changes in plain language.
- Faster onboarding and code reviews due to searchable, up-to-date change summaries.
- Reduced manual documentation overhead, with clear metrics on processing volume and success rates.
- A phased rollout plan: quick operational improvements first, followed by performance and accuracy upgrades, then advanced team and enterprise capabilities.
